text.title: Bug Report Quality Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every incoming bug report must satisfy before triage. The rubric scorer enforces them mechanically; the rules are HARD — reporter prose cannot override them.

rule.statement: Reproduction steps start from a named, observable initial state (URL, env, user role) and are numbered 1..N with one atomic action per step.

rationale.source: QA Wolf — What makes a great bug report; testgrid.io advanced guide 2026

rule.rationale: Repro paragraphs without a known entry state force the developer to reverse-engineer setup. Numbered atomic steps eliminate ambiguity about which action caused the observed state.

rule.statement: Every bug report carries at least one piece of visual or log evidence (screenshot, screen-recording, console excerpt, HAR file, structured error log).

rationale.source: Bird Eats Bug — Bug Report Writing 101

rule.rationale: Text-only reports drop crucial visual / temporal signal. Attached evidence converts "doesn't work" into a falsifiable observation.

rule.statement: Severity (technical impact) and priority (business urgency) are recorded as two separate fields with values from the team's matrix; they MAY differ.

rationale.source: Bird Eats Bug — Bug Severity vs Priority

rule.rationale: Conflating the two means a typo on the checkout page (low severity, high priority) gets the same triage as a logout race (high severity, possibly low priority for an internal beta). The two-field shape forces the conversation that gets the right fix order.

rule.statement: Environment block lists OS+version, browser+version (if web), build/SHA, feature-flag state, and user role/permissions — never "latest" or "main".
rule.rationale: "Latest" is a moving target; six weeks later "latest" is a different binary. Pin the build SHA so future-you can git-bisect.

rule.statement: If the failing surface is an LLM/AI feature, the report MUST include prompt id (or full prompt), model id+version, temperature/seed, full input conversation, and the verbatim output observed.

rationale.source: QA Wolf — AI testing addendum; internal lesson from neromedia LLM debug logs

rule.rationale: LLM outputs are non-deterministic; without prompt+model+seed the developer cannot reproduce. Paraphrased prompts and "GPT said X" reports are unfixable.
